description_long: This is a classic black rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust, angular base and a separate handset resting on top. The handset is connected to the base by a coiled cord, which is thick and springy, typical of mid-20th-century telephones. The front of the base displays a circular rotary dial with finger holes and visible numbers, surrounded by a metallic ring. The cradle on top securely holds the handset, with two protruding hooks that depress when the handset is in place. The surface of the phone is glossy and hard, reflecting light and showing some subtle wear and smudges. The overall construction suggests a combination of hard plastic and metal, with internal components likely including wiring and air gaps.
description: A vintage black rotary dial corded telephone with a glossy finish, coiled cord, and prominent handset cradle. The base is angular and sturdy, with a circular rotary dial on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, number plate, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the handset and the coiled cord extending from the base.
description_view_2: Side/rear view displaying the full length of the coiled cord and the smooth, glossy surface of the base.
description_view_3: Angled side view showing the rotary dial, handset, and the coiled cord looping outward.
